fallback_messages: The repo log has a useful constraint: for this build, direct JS `click()` inside `eval` is more reliable than `agent-browser click` on several game buttons. I’m switching to targeted DOM probes inside the active Labor Sector dashboard so I can handle the transit condition and run only verified actions.

Completed one bounded Week 2 slice for Persona A from the live `phase11-safe-grinder` continuity session. The authoritative post-week state is `Turn 3`, `Hab-Pod 404`, `₡345`, `Hunger 40`, `Sanity 27`, `Time 22CH`, and the Week 2 checkpoint was exported to [week-02-save.json](/home/silwerdivan/code/jones-vibes/docs/workflows/cyberpunk-overhaul/checkpoints/persona_a/week-02-save.json) with metadata in [week-02-meta.json](/home/silwerdivan/code/jones-vibes/docs/workflows/cyberpunk-overhaul/checkpoints/persona_a/week-02-meta.json).

The main gameplay finding is that the live session did not start on the stale Week 1 boundary described in the docs; it resumed mid-Week 2 at Labor Sector with no active `Transit Strike` condition. `WORK SHIFT` still no-oped under automation, so the slice fell back to `Scrap Metal`, which immediately chained into a hidden `Tetanus-Grade Laceration` event and collapsed the displayed `+₡85` fallback into a net `+₡5` before the forced `Patch it up` penalty.
